name: GFC Credit Crisis
notes: Global financial crisis triggered by US subprime mortgage collapse. Fed emergency cuts from 5.25% to 0.25%. VN severely impacted: inflation surged to 28% in 2008, SBV hiked rates aggressively then reversed.

name: QE1 Response and Recovery
notes: Fed launches QE1 ($1.75T MBS/Treasuries). US economy stabilizes. Labor market lags — unemployment peaks at 10% Oct 2009. VN stimulus package supports 5.3% GDP growth in 2009.

name: European Debt Crisis / QE2
notes: Greece/Portugal/Spain sovereign crises. Fed launches QE2 ($600B). ZIRP maintained. VN facing twin deficits: inflation 18.7% in 2011, SBV forced aggressive tightening. VND devalued multiple times.

name: QE3 and Recovery
notes: Fed launches open-ended QE3 ($85B/month), adds forward guidance. US recovery strengthens. Taper tantrum May 2013. VN inflation cooling — SBV gradually cuts rates to support credit growth.

name: Taper and First Rate Hike
notes: QE3 taper complete Oct 2014. First Fed hike Dec 2015 (ZIRP ends after 7 years). Oil price collapse dampens inflation. China devaluation shockwave Aug 2015. VN low inflation, SBV holds rates stable.

name: Gradual Tightening
notes: Fed hikes quarterly (25bp each). Balance sheet reduction begins Oct 2017. US tax reform Dec 2017 boosts growth. VN strong export-led growth; SBV keeps rates stable supporting domestic expansion.

name: Late-Cycle Rate Plateau
notes: Fed pauses after Dec 2018 hike (peak 2.5%). Markets sell off Q4 2018 on rate fears. US-China trade war escalates. Yield curve inverts Mar 2019. VN benefits from trade diversion but trade war uncertainty weighs.

name: Rate Cut Pivot 2019
notes: Fed cuts rates 3x (insurance cuts) Jul-Oct 2019 citing global uncertainty. Repo market stress Sep 2019. US manufacturing weakens. VN CPI rises on pork shortage (ASF). SBV cuts refinancing rate Oct 2019.

name: COVID Crisis
notes: Pandemic emergency: Fed cuts to 0-0.25% at emergency meetings Mar 3 and Mar 15. Unemployment spikes to 14.7% Apr 2020. CARES Act $2.2T. VN controlled spread well; GDP barely contracted in 2020.

name: COVID Recovery + QE Infinity
notes: Vaccine rollout begins Dec 2020. Fed: unlimited QE, average inflation targeting (AIT) framework adopted Aug 2020. $120B/month asset purchases. VN GDP recovers to 2.9% in 2020; export boom. SBV cuts again to support.

name: Reflation and Reopening
notes: US reopening boom. Inflation picks up but Fed insists 'transitory'. Stimulus cheques + supply chain bottlenecks. VN hit by Delta wave; prolonged lockdowns Aug-Oct 2021 sharply dent H2 GDP.

name: Inflation Surge
notes: Fed abandons 'transitory' Dec 2021. Taper accelerated. First 2022 hike Mar (+25bp). Russia invades Ukraine Feb — energy/food shock. CPI peaks 9.1% Jun 2022. VN inflation contained; SBV holds rates.

name: Aggressive Tightening
notes: Fed hikes 75bp four consecutive times (Jun, Jul, Sep, Nov 2022). Balance sheet reduction $95B/month. Dollar surges — DXY peaks 114. Crypto collapse, VC funding freeze. VN dong pressure; SBV hikes twice in Oct.

name: Terminal Rate Plateau
notes: Fed reaches terminal rate 5.25-5.5% Jul 2023. SVB/Signature Bank collapse Mar 2023 — banking stress. Inflation decelerating but above target. VN property market stress; SBV cuts rates 4x in 2023 to support growth.

name: Soft Landing Debate
notes: US economy surprisingly resilient — no recession. Markets price in multiple 2024 cuts but expectations repeatedly pushed back. PCE inflation sticky. VN GDP rebounds to 5%+ with export recovery. VN30 strong H2 2023 rally.

name: Rate Cut Pivot 2024
notes: Fed cuts 50bp Sep 2024 (first cut since 2020), then 25bp Nov and Dec. Total 100bp in 2024. Market debate: insurance cuts or cycle start? VN dong under pressure from USD strength despite Fed cuts.

name: New Regime 2025
notes: Fed pauses cutting amid tariff-driven inflation uncertainty. Trump 2.0 trade policy: 25% tariffs on Canada/Mexico, China escalation. Markets pricing stagflation risk. VN export sector faces headwinds but FDI inflows strong.
